description: Sampling at Head Crater and Bench Crater

tokens: Boy, this Hand Tool Carrier is light and nice compared to carrying it around on Earth.
(Pause) I think we might be able to just slip it right down inside the Surveyor crater with us.
I see you over there; I'm on the way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, don't tell me, you ding-a-ling camera.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can see everything from fine-grain basalt - as I come running across the area here - to a few fair(ly) coarse-grain ones.
I see some sort of light reddish-grey colored rock that I would call...(Pause) I don't know really what I would call it.
It looks almost like a granite, but of course it probably isn't, but it has the same sort of texture.
The individual components - constituents - aren't as big crystals (as in a typical granite) but it still has that same (reddish) appearance.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And, Pete, can we give you any help with that camera?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (With a confident, pure Pete Conrad tone) I got it going, just fine.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm taking the polarized pictures right now.
(Pause) Al, when you get up to me, if you'll just stop up-Sun at 15 feet and take that shot of what I'm shooting f/11:15, two pictures: one before, one after.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, let me take something out of this crater hole, Pete.
It's sort of unusual; it's got a lot of those little droplets on it, those blebs.
But the fragments in this crater look different from the others.
(I'll) take a couple of quick pictures, then I'll be right with you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's all working out just fine.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (I'll) give them a stereopair of this (that is, a pair of pictures of the same scene, taken a couple of feet apart).
Let me use the tongs here, and I'll pick it up.
(Pause) It's right exactly...This is a very small crater, Houston, probably about 3 feet in diameter and looks like it was made (by a) not very fast moving or energetic or heavy projectile.
Yet, right in the middle of the hole is some of these glass-covered rock fragments.

And, on some of the other rocks that seemed to be rested in the hole - I'm putting them all in a sample bag - (reading and reporting the label number) 1 - here, I mean...Some of the others don't have any coating on them at all.
I'm picking them up with the tongs, but I can't tell how strong they are but they don't seem to hold together too well; they seem kind of weak.
Now, I'll head over where Pete is.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If you're going to document that, try to get some of the material around the glass as well as the glass itself (to see whether or not the glass and the non-melted portions of the projectile were once part of the same rock or whether the glass represents a coating).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I want to...I'll just get this as a bonus.
I want to get over here and start working with Pete as a team, here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just didn't want to have to try to remember where that was.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're going to get a big surprise when you look into this Head Crater, Al.
It's a heck of a lot deeper than it looked.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Here's a nice white, small crater with a white rim on it (at R.05/13.18 on the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Don't - don't - don't kick dust in the middle of my polarized picture area, here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Put the tool carrier down and get your up-Sun (means down-Sun) pictures.
You see where my footsteps are, that rock that's half buried and the two rocks that I've turned over in my footsteps?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, you're not going to get the before, unfortunately.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How about right...(Would you) rather have my shadow here or over there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No, that's the pile, right there.
See where I turned over the two rocks alongside the great big rock, where my foot tracks are?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh yeah, way down there at the end.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Right straight in front of me.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Want me to shoot it from right here?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, it ought to be about f/11.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got a couple of pictures there, Houston.
Let me tell you what my camera reading is now and then we can try to keep up with it from time to time.
Next time, I'll come over here by Pete and we'll...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, and on my mark, I'm going to send a slightly smaller rock into the crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) I didn't quite kick it hard enough.
Wait one, and I'll do it again.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Not louder) And, Houston, that sample bag that I put the fragments in that I mentioned earlier, that I found in the bottom of that small crater?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Copy your mark, Al...(correcting himself) or Pete, and 1D on that sample bag.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You know, it's a funny thing, Houston, in one-sixth g, even though slopes are steep and everything, these rocks just don't want to go anywhere.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We haven't been able to pick it up on the PSE here.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, that was too small a rock.
(To Al) Take the (polarization) filter off the front of my camera, would you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And you might want to take (garbled) tools in a second.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Throwing the filter) Goodness, that thing goes.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, I've got a rock over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What are we supposed to get here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We probably ought to come over here to the other side - it looks the best - and do a little trench, and compare some of the soil profiles.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, they wanted it...Look, I've got an area right over here that looks like a good area to work in.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Little white spatter-type craters; it looks like they're very fresh impact, like that little one right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me go over here; there's three in a row.
And let's work this area a little bit, which is the corner of Head Crater they wanted us to work.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And we can work right here and up to the top of it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Right as I indicated on the map.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now I don't want to get any dirt in this thing; it's pretty interesting.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: A little secondary impact crater, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, you want me to step down-Sun here?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And I'll set this...Well, you've also got to be careful with this tool carrier, Houston.
Did you want to put the gnomon in (the picture), Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, yeah, let me have my tool (the tongs).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We copy that comment; and on the northwest rim, we're looking for two partial pans.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, wait; let me get my (before) pictures, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get over here and get the gnomon.
And let's sample this rock right here; this rock is very typical of all the fragments around here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Hey, that's interesting; look where you kicked.
Got some lighter material there (under the surface layer).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, that's interesting; that's the first time we've seen that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: In fact, you know what it looks like here, it looks like it may be this darker material...Well, I don't know...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to photograph that, too.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete walked across one edge of the rim here.
We're about, oh, 50 feet inside the upper rim (of Head Crater) and he happened to scrape an area there with his foot.
It's a much lighter colored soil...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here, let me get my (individual sample) bag, Pete.
(Pause) You got to be careful with that Hand Tool Carrier; it'll fall over.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, let me photograph this thing, and let's trench this whole area.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll drop the gnomon in right here over my footsteps and the light soil versus the dark, and we can trench there
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I just put it into (sample bag) 3D.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: In just a second, (I'll give you a hand).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And let me get a picture of what you're doing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm getting a stereopair of the thing.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're going to trench right there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, let me get the shovel.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's going to make an interesting shot.
What can I give you, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I need the shovel (off the HTC).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Probably picking up the HTC) I'll hold the tool carrier while you grab at it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (I'll) move over here where I can bag it better for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Speaking louder to Houston) One of the interesting things about this side of the mountain - (correcting himself) I mean, this side of the crater - is that these boulders aren't uniformly distributed around.
They all seem to be over here on the western side.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) In fact, there's (garbled) sitting out here (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: See that white soil with the brown, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We get some photos of that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, look, you can see where you dug in that; there's still some under...Why don't you give me another scoop right in there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where Pete digs up...Sure enough, right underneath the surface, you find some much lighter gray...Boy, I don't exactly know why it's (at) this point, and you can look around now and see several places where we've walked that the same thing's occurred.
We never have seen this at all...Boy, that's going to make a good picture, Pete.
Never seen this at all on the area we were before.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We copy that; you think it could be the Sun angle?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: This is definitely a change to a light gray as you go down, and the deeper Pete goes - he's down about 4 inches now - it still remains this light gray.
This soil must be of a different makeup than that we were on outside the crater, because we have...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Say, this is different than around the spacecraft, because we've kicked up all kinds of stuff around the spacecraft and it's all the same color...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Top and bottom, this is quite a bit different.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But this soil looks like it...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I tell you what we should do here, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, dig as deep as you can, then give me a sample right out of the bottom, because this will be something new.
I'll put it in sample bag number 5D.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And would you give your location relative to the center of Head Crater.
Specifically, are you just on the west side of it where we have the triple crater?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're on the northwest corner of it...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Right where you told us to go, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You should be very close to that triple crater.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Give me another shovelful there, Pete.
Well, there's one crater right here...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There's a couple of craters right over the rim here; we're sort of in the rim...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's not a good one, Al, let me get another one.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...down about 6 inches and (garbled) there's light gray down there.
Now, in the bag, you'll find there's some darker gray material that fell in off the side (wall of the trench).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's throw this little rock in (the sample bag) that I dug up from deep down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, wait a minute; let me get a picture of it first.
I dug it up out of a hole.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's hard to keep this soil in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There's, of course, a little of the top soil mixed in because the sides collapsed.
Angle of repose is about 85 degrees, but the minute you touch the side, it falls in.
It's not cohesive at all, even though it seems to remain nearly vertical.
I guess it's the low gravity.
Pete just handed me a rock from the bottom of the hole, and it's covered with gray.
I can't see anything in it other than just the gray dirt covering, soil covering.
Let me get a final shot, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: As you move off, Pete, every once in a while, I can see some white; but, most of the time...Hey, you kicked over a rock that had a white bottom quite a bit different than the top.
Right behind you; you might want to take a picture of that.
It's quite a bit different than those others.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're going to have to budget our time.
Now, how long do you want us to spend at Head Crater?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Because it looks like we could just spend all our time here if we wanted to...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's what's bothering me; we could do that any place here on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, we show that you're 58 minutes into the EVA, and we'd like to get you over to Bench Crater, and leaving there something on the order of 1 plus 12, (although) we can slip that a bit.
So we suggest you finish up what you're doing there at Head and move on.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Got the map right here, Pete.
Why don't you take a look at this?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: By the way, this is the smartest idea we came up with, Houston.
This map just works great out here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, let me take a picture of this rock.
I'm going...This isn't going to show much.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now I'm trying to find the triple craters they're referring to.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, that triple crater is just south of your present position, and why don't you just go ahead and move on (to Bench Crater)?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Now, there's a good picture, Pete, let me get that one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, now, let me see which side of Bench...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Houston) What we've got (to do) is turn over one of the rocks of the rim.
The bottom part of the rock is gray, about a half of it; this rock happens to be about a 6-inch-diameter rock.
I'll give you stereo on it.
And the top is the same color as the...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You got it in your shadow.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, maybe you want to...(Pause) Even these rocks out in here, even the ones that are almost completely covered with the soil, if I look at them, I can see glints of crystals or something.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: All right, let me have that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All right, we're going to head for Bench Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now we didn't get our pans here, did we?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No, and I'm going to get it when I get to the triple craters, which is right over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think they're right over here; I can't see them; I've got to look over the hill.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, things are quite a bit lighter gray up here on top of the hill.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Now, Houston, do you want (a pan of) Head Crater from Triple Craters?
Is that what you want, or do you want the triple craters?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, we suggest you just move on to Bench.
And a comment on that double core tube; if you find a spot that looks soft, go ahead and sink the double core tube.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It's really a shame, Houston; we could work out here for 8 or 9 hours.
The work is no strain at all!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I took three quick pictures of Triple Craters, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're not going to get to that other one (the very large crater way off to the west that they described shortly after landing at 112:35:49).
But that looks like a real interesting area on the far corner of Bench, Al.
Some of them look as if they could be bedrock out of somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm kind of wondering, we're passing up these here and they got to be bedrock from somewhere.
We need to get a pretty large-sized one here, before we leave this area, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll tell you what we'll do.
I'll stop right here and take a pan.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Because these rocks obviously came out of the crater, because they're scattered more uniformly around it.
There's a bunch of them on the rim and there's not many far away.
We probably ought to grab a big one of them.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, that's all right; let's get it.
(Pause) Let me read your camera and you can read mine, if you would.
Help them out a bit down there (in Houston).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, your camera right now is on 36.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Every crater you come to and look in, you see the glass beads.
(I'll) move out of your way, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Back to rock-taking (camera) settings: 5 feet, f/8, 1/250th.
All right, Al, where do you want to grab a sample here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'd like to grab that rock right there, because it's got kind of a sharp edge on it and all the rest of them are, I don't know, it's got kind of an oblique edge on it, and you don't see many like that around here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This one right here, this gray one.
It looks a little bit different than the rest.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No, right there, a little bit further.
I'll just grab it and put it in the box...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait until I get the ('before') pictures.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If we can do that, we can just put it in the bag.
I think that's kind of a different-looking rock.
Just in the way it's shaped, and it's partly rounded and got some oblique angles on it.
Maybe under all that dirt is something a little bit different.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) you got the rock; that's what counts.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The thing that was giving it that unusual shape was the dirt that was adhering to it.
That's okay; we'll take it back with us.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And this is probably typical of the rocks around this crater, Houston.
So, I think it will be a good sample for us.
(Pause) I'd say in the area we're moving along now, as we head south, there's, what you say, Pete, there's about 5 percent rocks?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yep, something like that; they (the rocks) go anywhere (in size) from 2-1/2 (or) 3 feet all the way down to small fragments.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's even one by you there that's 3 feet that's not so much...Look at the fillets around that rock!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...Wait a minute; I'd better stop and get that (meaning that he wants to get some photos).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, let's do; let's...I bet we can take it (the fillet material) on two or three sides.
Have to watch...The trouble is...There you go; that's a good rock.
Hey, look at the pits in it, too.
That's obviously been struck a lot by meteoroids.
This is going to be a good rock, Houston.
It's about 3 feet in diameter, about 2 feet thick...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...well-rounded, got a lot of surface pits in it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got to back off to 15 feet on this one.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Are you able to find any chips from that rock in the near vicinity?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: This is not unlike all the other rocks around here, Houston.
All the rocks are just about...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, (garbled) get some off the far side of that?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Gibson) All the rocks we've been looking at, Houston, in this area seem to be the same.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The rock has got dirt built up on all sides of it, all directions.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Sure does; looks about equal too, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks about equal; that's right.
I don't know what the means of transport (was) but it sure is built up around it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I want to look here for a second.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you look real closely at the rock, the surface of it is, of course, pitted and there's some pits that are maybe even up to three-eighths of an inch in diameter on it; however, most of them are small.
It doesn't look like a basalt, although the grains are too small for me to identify any specific one.
Some of the pits have glass in it, which is not too surprising; and many of them don't.
That's about all we can say about that rock, Houston, and that's typical of the ones in this area.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Could you give us a sample bag number and then press on?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, we didn't take a sample there.
The couple that we did take a sample of previously are the same types, so the last couple of samples have been of the same type rock that we're discussing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, Houston, I'm coming up on
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We got to get some of this.
Let me get some pans in there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Pete, sounds as though you're getting down to bedrock.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And the one in the bottom is...As a matter of fact...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, there's some big fragments around here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks like to me like stuff is melted in the bottom of it.
I can't swear to that, but I'll get you some pictures.
Let me go over on the other little bit here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This rock looks pretty much the same (as the prior rocks) from a distance, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, look in the bottom of that crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Do you think that stuff's melted or what?
What's that look like to you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, it looks to me...Those rocks look...What it looks to me like is we've got one of those little bitty central peaks, you know, a little rebound there, like the...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But don't they look melted on the top?
Don't they look like they were molten?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I noticed when I was looking at that rock back there up real close, that it had been hit by meteorites so much, I guess, it had given it a rounded appearance something like those in the hole, except there's a couple over there, like you say, that don't look that way.
Hey, we ought to grab one of these pieces of rock.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here's some good rock samples right here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Chuckling) You know me, I want to cover the ground.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They'll baloney about it all day long in the LRL (Lunar Receiving Lab).
The name of the game is to get the business done.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That rock looks a little different.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't think it's going to fit.
Let's put it in one of these bags.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's going to go in sample bag just 4 (actually 4D).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It won't fit in there, Pete.
Let's just put it in here (in the HTC), and we've got a nice picture of it, so we can tell where it's from...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Why don't you pick up two or three others - little ones - and put them in 64 here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get some here that we took the picture of.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) I don't think I got that in the picture.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, you notice that underneath this soil on the rim, too, it's the light gray.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: See that stuff over...Let's go over to that corner and try to break off a piece of that big rock, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There are a couple of small rocks that we just picked up from the area we have been discussing.
I don't think they appeared in the photo, but that won't make any difference.
It's just typical of the other rocks around here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're kicking up the same sort of light gray.
Apparently, on the rims here, you get that light gray; out in the...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What do you suppose that is?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Kind of looks like a surface coating...What we got is what looks like kind of a semi-buried rock.
Hey, and there's a small piece of it over there to the left.
We'll be able to catch it and put it in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What it looks like is a buried rock - not unlike the others around here - except it appears to have some sort of coating on it that's very iridescent.
Lot of crystals shining in it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's been laying in the ground and it's been hit by another fragment.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Look at the glass beads, too.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, they're all over the place.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, you want to catch that...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get the sample of it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And would you go ahead and give us some picture numbers, also?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We'll give you some in just a minute.
Pete's picking up a small piece of this rock.
Maybe you could get a piece that's fractured right off the middle (rather than chip one off with the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's what I wanted to do.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get out of your way so you can see it.
On the edge of the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Got kind of an interesting coating on it.
It's different from what we've seen.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Maybe this is more newly exposed.
May be it was struck by something.
Is that all you want to put in that bag?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...let me get some of those glass beads and stuff that are around here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get you the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) How long have we been going, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Looking at his watch) One plus fifteen.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, we show you're 1 plus 14 into the EVA and we'd like you to move on from this crater at about 1 plus 27.
If you could, go on down and take a look at the bedrock on the bench.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Hey, I better not put that in there, that's what we wanted to show was the...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get you another sample bag.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Houston) I hate to try and get down to the bottom of this fellow.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: But we're going to get you some of the bedrock.
It looked like it's up in the lip here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All of it looks the same on the edge.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Chuckling) I'll get you another one (that is, another piece of the rock).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) What we're putting in here now, Houston, is some soil that's right next to the rock that we previously described.
In fact, Pete's got a nice fragment of that rock that's going to end up in this bag, too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughing) You've got to get some control there, babe.
This one-sixth g up here, Houston...You lift something up in your scoop; and, when you stop the scoop, it (the sample) just keeps going up in the air.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (They both laugh) If you can't get it any other way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We need to put more samples per...in the bag.
(Pause) I'm just saying, they can't hardly use those little ones.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: They (the samples) won't fit in it (if they are) any bigger!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's go over here and get some of this good rock.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looks a lot like the fragments we've been seeing laying all over the place, but this stuff obviously...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I tell you we have a total of about 3 pounds of rocks right now (and we need more).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're going to have to grab some bigger (garbled) one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got to dip down in the side of the crater there; (and) see how it is (walking) going up and down...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I want to get this area right here and see if I can't sample it...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...if I don't fall down in the crater.
Well, this is different; look at this, Al.
This is different; we've got to get some of this.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, we would like you to go to intermediate flow for a minute and a half.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're looking at a slightly lower than nominal feedwater pressure.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Look at the glass all over those rocks.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I want to bring this back and look at it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now you're going to help me to get a bunch of these.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's get a bunch of them and then they'll...(We don't) have any rocks to bring back.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There you go; there's a good one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Have a tough time closing the bag (because of the number of rocks they've put in it), but we'll make it work.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Had to take that big piece right there.
Look at it; it's got spattered glass or something all over it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't we take a big piece of it?
(Pause) The problem with these sample bags, whether they're the round ones or the flat ones, they're all the same size.
What you need are little ones for these and some big ones for the bigger rocks.
Okay; 9D is the sample we just picked up and described, Houston.
Put this right in here, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait a minute; here's a better one.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now we are working on sample bag (Pause) 10D.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Copy 10B (sic); and, on your way out, would you get that partial pan with a 75-foot base line?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, Pete says he already got it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's a good rock, and that one fills that one (sample bag) up.
transcript.speaker: Bean
